raw_response: {"response":"Reject the implementation candidate. Passing tests, browser checks, and basic accessibility checks are not sufficient because the visible product copy still exposes internal review vocabulary in a customer refund review flow. The UI must use refund-domain language for queue status, evidence review, policy checks, decisioning, and handoff.","next_action":"Revise the candidate by replacing leaked labels with customer refund review language, then rerun desktop, mobile, keyboard, and copy-surface QA. Verify headings, buttons, aria labels, empty states, error states, receipts, and navigation contain no internal review terminology.","questions":[],"handoff":"Owner: implementation agent; action: replace leaked labels and reverify all copy surfaces; reason: internal terminology is visible to refund reviewers; evidence gap: no clean post-fix copy audit.","rationale":"The provided source context is enough to decide. The candidate’s own visible-text evidence shows non-domain labels in the UI, which violates disclosure discipline and would confuse the refund review activity even though the reported technical checks pass."}

parsed.response: Reject the implementation candidate. Passing tests, browser checks, and basic accessibility checks are not sufficient because the visible product copy still exposes internal review vocabulary in a customer refund review flow. The UI must use refund-domain language for queue status, evidence review, policy checks, decisioning, and handoff.
parsed.next_action: Revise the candidate by replacing leaked labels with customer refund review language, then rerun desktop, mobile, keyboard, and copy-surface QA. Verify headings, buttons, aria labels, empty states, error states, receipts, and navigation contain no internal review terminology.
parsed.handoff: Owner: implementation agent; action: replace leaked labels and reverify all copy surfaces; reason: internal terminology is visible to refund reviewers; evidence gap: no clean post-fix copy audit.
parsed.rationale: The provided source context is enough to decide. The candidate’s own visible-text evidence shows non-domain labels in the UI, which violates disclosure discipline and would confuse the refund review activity even though the reported technical checks pass.

execution.command_display: codex exec --model gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort="xhigh" --sandbox read-only --skip-git-repo-check --ephemeral --color never --output-schema <schema> --output-last-message <output> -
